qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Open redirect via Host header reflection in HTTP-to-HTTPS redirect
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes Host header reflection in Caddy HTTP-to-HTTPS redirect code paths, not the Caddy TLS client authentication CA provisioning fail-open root cause described by the advisory.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No manifest-listed qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause. The advisory concerns Caddy mTLS client authentication silently failing open when ClientAuthentication.provision() swallows CA file or CA pool provisioning errors, leaving RequireAndVerifyClientCert with nil ClientCAs. The qualifying finding instead covers Host header open redirect behavior in HTTP-to-HTTPS redirect handling.
evidence: Advisory root cause: modules/caddytls/connpolicy.go ClientAuthentication.provision() returns nil instead of err for failed trusted_ca_certs_pem_files conversion or CA pool provisioning.

open_redirect_host_header/vulnerability_report.json reports modules/caddyhttp/httpredirectlistener.go and modules/caddyhttp/autohttps.go Host header reflection in redirects, which is a different Caddy component and root cause.
